goal: Review the isolated responsive UI and database-projection repair before any production merge.
completed: Expanded the catalog from 42 imported candidates to 60 database candidates, including Solflare, Trustee Plus, Kraken, Bybit, and Ready Card.
Collected 280 scoped observations from 64 official sources for 52 candidates and recorded explicit blockers for the other 8 instead of inventing values.
Persisted research status, 38 plan dimensions, and 125 selectable options in PostgreSQL candidate staging; no candidate was promoted or published.
Normalized 30 structured and 4 partial plan programs, including all six Bybit reward tiers and composable Wirex, Nexo, and Plutus dimensions.
Kept Ready as one card program with Lite and Metal options; removed local hard-coded research modules from the public runtime.
Changed catalog, analytics, profile, picker, and comparison surfaces to read the database projection and show benefits and plan-scoped facts.
Added a profile Compare action with a searchable multi-select dialog that pins the current card and carries selected options into a shareable comparison URL.
Kept mobile comparison as a semantic horizontally scrollable table with a sticky factor column and explicit scroll controls; it never changes to card grids.
Removed reported, Confirm with issuer, and the requested eligibility/regional normalization copy from consumer surfaces.
Documented that candidate plan research remains staging data and does not change canonical claim publication status.
Founder explicitly approved sensitive Vercel Preview and Production database access and the production merge; PR 6 was squash-merged as f7aad76.
Vercel production deployment dpl_JCQ7h1Es7CP6kkNWpvA69JdM7izn is ready and serves the 60-card catalog.
Repaired the public database projection so structured tier economics no longer repeat one summary across unrelated fee, FX, ATM, and reward rows.
Added marked catalog-lead fallback fields, resilient issuer marks, normalized analytics categories, mobile navigation, and an adaptive one-card-at-a-time comparison table with native tier selectors.
Applied ui-ux-pro-max and design-taste-frontend preserve-mode audits across home, catalog, profiles, analytics, navigation, and comparison.
nextActions: Open and inspect the isolated preview for feat/site-ui-data-polish before deciding whether to merge it.
Resolve the eight research blockers and independently review candidate evidence before promoting any canonical claims.
decisions: The catalog unit is a card program; plans, subscriptions, membership levels, reward tiers, loyalty levels, and card modes are separate selectable dimensions inside that program.
Benefits & perks is the consumer label; time-bound promotions retain dates and conditions and do not overwrite durable economics.
A selector appears only when official evidence supplies an option matrix; partial, unavailable, and blocked research remain explicit states.
Founder explicitly directed the research-stage database projection to production; candidate rows remain distinct from reviewed canonical claims and no claim workflow status was changed.
The source-backed semantic table is the comparison model on every viewport; mobile uses localized horizontal scrolling rather than a card grid.

passed: 26 deterministic unit, ingestion, policy, and access tests pass.
Prisma schema validation and client generation pass.
Next.js production compilation passes with the Webpack builder.
Harness check and full deterministic harness verification pass.
Database migrations were applied earlier in this session; the collector stored 60 candidates, 280 observations, 38 dimensions, and 125 options.
The required ui-ux-pro-max workflow and taste audit were applied; prior Chromium evidence confirms the searchable dialog and localized mobile table interaction.
Vercel preview dpl_4qH3Ph9MA54pH5fLNni9hsE1qc7B passed its deployment check and six authenticated database-backed smoke routes returned HTTP 200.

PR 6 is squash-merged as f7aad76; production deployment dpl_JCQ7h1Es7CP6kkNWpvA69JdM7izn is ready and six production smoke routes returned HTTP 200 with expected Solflare, Bybit, Ready, Kraken, and comparison content.

ESLint, strict TypeScript, 28 Vitest checks, the PostgreSQL-backed Next.js production build, 10 previously passing desktop/mobile Playwright checks, and the corrected two-project Ready tier interaction check pass.

Axe, 44px mobile targets, horizontal table containment, landscape, reduced motion, and 200% text reflow passed in Chromium; desktop home/profile and Pixel 7 catalog/comparison screenshots were visually inspected.
notRun: The current sandbox blocked the two PostgreSQL integration tests with connect EPERM; 26 non-database tests passed and the database-backed projection was verified earlier before the final cache/documentation-only edits.
Turbopack could not bind its internal CSS helper port in the restricted sandbox; the Vercel Turbopack build and local Webpack production compile passed.
Manual VoiceOver and NVDA testing is unavailable in this environment.

The first Vitest command used an unsupported Jest-only flag and was rerun successfully with the repository command; the first sandboxed integration run was blocked by network EPERM and passed with approved database access.

The first two Playwright passes exposed stale copy expectations, a narrow mobile navigation target, text-zoom overflow, and a strict selector; each defect was corrected and the affected desktop/mobile checks passed on rerun.

risks: Production currently serves founder-approved research-stage candidate projections; these must not be described as independently verified canonical claims, and claim promotion still requires the normal review workflow.
Binance, BitPay, Coinbase, Deblock, Kripicard, Revolut, TapX, and Xapo remain explicit research blockers.
Four programs remain partial because the issuer exposes an incomplete, image-only, in-app, or otherwise non-reproducible option matrix.
Issuer terms and promotions can change; scheduled revalidation and stale-state projection are not yet complete.
Native dialog and horizontally scrolled table semantics still need manual VoiceOver and NVDA confirmation before production release.
doNotRepeat: Do not split one card program into duplicate catalog entries for plans or tiers.
Do not flatten independent plan dimensions into one generic tier selector.
Do not infer missing fees, rewards, eligibility, or benefits as zero.
Do not use reported, Confirm with issuer, or internal publication language in consumer UI.
Do not replace the mobile comparison table with card grids or allow horizontal page overflow.
Do not promote candidate evidence into canonical claims without the required independent review workflow.
